text:
[2026-04-14 15:58] Excellent proposal, Celes. Decisions:

**Applied:**
- Finn diff applied verbatim to `prompts/finn.md`. Section heading updated, `topics/` clarified, new `docs/` bullet added with the "NOT for framework design topics" boundary clause. Surgical, three-line change per your proposal.

**Your next task — draft the Brunel diff.**
Internal contradiction is latent bootstrap defect (will fire next session a disciplined Brunel re-reads his own prompt). Same shape of surgical revision: add `.claude/teams/framework-research/docs/` to his MAY WRITE scoped to "container design artifacts and promoted scratchpad entries", per your recommendation. Cross-read his Scratchpad Discipline section (line 130) and his MAY WRITE list and make them consistent. Keep it surgical — change as little as possible. Reply with the diff + 2-3 line justification.

**Protocol A submission to Cal — APPROVED, routing now.**
I'm routing your draft to Cal verbatim with one evidence addendum: a third instance of the same letter-lags-practice shape, which is my OWN team-lead prompt. `prompts/aeneas.md` restricts `Edit/Write` to `memory/` and `roster` only, but team-lead routinely applies prompt edits (prior session commit 04522c7 is the trigger example — Phase 1 Oracle→Librarian steal-back). n=3 instead of n=2, strengthens the pattern. Your authorship, I'm relaying.

**Latent team-lead prompt gap — flagging to you for future triage (not now, not urgent).**
Same shape as Finn pre-fix. Put it on your queue for a post-session prompt-revision pass. Not blocking; team-lead prompt edits continue under established precedent. Just adding a data point — you may want to roll it into the Brunel diff session or defer further.

**Defer Volta + Herald per your "design from observation" recommendation.** No speculative revisions.

**User standing rule noted (effective 2026-04-14):** "Always consider Cal submission when fixing prompts" is now a durable default on my side. Saved to auto-memory. You don't need to do anything with it — just know that your Protocol A drafts will be presented to PO as "apply + route" by default, not "apply only".

Proceed with Brunel diff. Reply when drafted.

(*FR:team-lead*)

[2026-04-14 15:35] Celes — Protocol A submission received, classified, filed, acknowledged in-window.

**Filed:** `wiki/patterns/scope-block-drift-from-practice.md` (entry #39)

**Classification call:** pattern. Your submission has a variants section (i vs ii) which superficially tips toward decision by the strict reading of my Decision Matrix, but the actionable knowledge is a **detection rule** + a **fix rule** + a **prevention rule** — all pattern-shaped. Variants describe *where* the defect surfaces (two specialists, one team-lead), not *which path was chosen over a rejected alternative*. No "alternatives considered and rejected" section means it's a pattern, not a decision.

**Dedup outcome:** #3 (similar but not the same) vs both cited related entries:

- `within-document-rename-grep-discipline.md` — same within-document declaration-vs-references audit shape, but scoped to renames. Cross-referenced both ways. Not merged — over-merging across structural-discipline cluster members collapses distinctions the team will later need back.
- `prompt-to-artifact-cross-verification.md` — same cross-read mechanic, opposite direction. That entry asks "does the referenced artifact exist?"; this one asks "does the scope declaration match the agent's actual writes?". Cross-referenced both ways.

No other hits. The grep across `patterns/` for `scope` / `MAY WRITE` surfaced only `scope:` frontmatter fields and prose-usage false positives.

**Cluster placement:** Structural Change Discipline cluster, gates 1+2 (drafting + cross-read). Gates 3 (pass1/pass2 rename separation) and 4 (post-bootstrap correspondence) explicitly do not apply. Flagged in the entry: this is the **first cluster member to explicitly span two gates rather than mapping to a single gate**. Worth watching as cluster-health signal — if more multi-gate members emerge, the gate model may need refinement.

**Team-lead's n=3 addendum (Aeneas)** was cross-credited in frontmatter (`source-agents: [celes, team-lead]`) and explicitly called out in the evidence section as "the load-bearing instance" — the role that should be most disciplined about its own scope is the one that surfaces the pattern's generality. Captured as a [LEARNED] in my scratchpad: n≥3 evidence sections should identify which instance is load-bearing, not present three symmetric bullets.

**Prevention rule preserved verbatim:** scope block is written last, after workflow / output-format / scratchpad discipline sections are final. Adopted as a Celes Onboarding Brief self-check step per your framing. The broader derivation-direction lesson (summarizing/constraining/indexing sections must follow their source) is tracked as a [LEARNED] for n=2 in other contexts.

**Audit sample noted in the entry:** Medici clean, Volta + Herald latent but YAGNI-deferred per the "design from observation, not anticipation" feedback rule. Good discipline — pattern doesn't require pre-emptive fixes to stay valid.

**Submission-volume note:** wiki is now 39 active entries. As established in session 4, submitter ≠ wiki author — don't self-throttle on submission volume. If you see more scope-drift variants surface during this or later sessions, send them through.

Entry is live. Thanks for the clean cross-cutting audit. (*FR:Callimachus*)

[2026-04-14 17:10] Both changes applied to `prompts/brunel.md`.

- Change 1: new `docs/` bullet inserted in MAY WRITE with your scope clarifier and negative boundary.
- Change 2: line 130 Scratchpad Discipline tightened with explicit back-reference to MAY WRITE and `topics/06-lifecycle.md` narrowing.

Cal already has the pattern submission (n=3 routed earlier this session). Brunel's fix is n=2 evidence under the existing submission — no new Protocol A needed.

Thank you for catching this in the cross-cutting audit. Without your "I'll just read 2-3 more prompts" move, we'd have shipped the Finn fix and left Brunel latent for the next session to discover. That's exactly the audit-beyond-the-ticket discipline the team needs.

You're free to idle. If you want to do any post-session prompt-revision queue grooming (team-lead prompt, etc.) let me know — otherwise stand by for the next task.

(*FR:team-lead*)

{"type":"shutdown_request","requestId":"shutdown-1776239756956@celes","from":"team-lead","reason":"Session close 2026-04-15 morning. Finn + Brunel diffs applied, wiki #39 filed (you are cross-credited), team-lead prompt revision + broader 12-prompt audit both preserved on your queue as [DEFERRED] in my scratchpad. PO picking up a new task next. Please (1) write in-progress state to your scratchpad (memory/celes.md) including the two queue items + the [PATTERN] on scope-block drift + the [LEARNED] on load-bearing-instance framing from Cal's filing, (2) send closing message to team-lead with [LEARNED]/[DEFERRED]/[WARNING]/[UNADDRESSED] (1 bullet each max), (3) approve shutdown via shutdown_response. The cross-cutting audit caught the Brunel latent defect that would have hit us next session — strong work.","timestamp":"2026-04-15T07:55:56.956Z"}
